title: Free Queen, Take It
setup: Two queens face off — but only one is defended.
hint: Always look for hanging pieces. The simplest tactic is *take the free thing*.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is the most basic **loose piece** tactic. The black queen on d4 is undefended. The white queen on d1 attacks her along the d-file. White just takes.

White plays {{Qxd4}}.

- The black king is on e8, far away — no recapture possible.
- No black piece defends d4.
- White wins a queen for nothing.

## Pattern to Memorize

> Before every move, scan for **undefended enemy pieces** on lines you control.

GM John Nunn called these *Loose Pieces* — the **LPDO** rule (Loose Pieces Drop Off) is the foundation of all tactics. Master this and the rest of tactical chess is just decoration.
